result: F-HOLO-4: spline curvature FIELD where the F-HOLO-3 constant-rotor exact solve is BLIND (0.520, chance) alongside trivial entropy (0.520), while a closed-form ChebyCR roughness readout reaches the oracle (1.000, no training); Laplacian 0.999 confirms it is not a Chebyshev artifact; MLP over raw edges 0.537.

NEW_EVALUATION_REQUIREMENT: a 3-way gate: trivial AND constant-rotor BOTH blind AND oracle clears — the exact-scalar-solve-insufficient regime
novelty_classification.note: User-proposed nonlinear curvature via ChebyCR patches; realized on the real spine (ChebyCR + rotor_holonomy), closed-form. Explicit NON-GOAL rejected: nonlinear along a 1-D edge (unidentifiable).

gates.ab: ChebyCR 1.000 reaches oracle where constant-rotor is blind; Laplacian 0.999; MLP 0.537
gates.perf: readout ~30 us/sample (<100 budget); example 2.3 s, 5.2 MB

integrity_note: The insufficiency claim (constant-rotor blind) is PROVED in code (matched mean flux across classes) not asserted; matched Haar marginals keep trivial entropy blind; Laplacian arm rules out a Chebyshev-basis artifact; plaquette=field and gauge-invariance are tested identities.
honest_scope: ChebyCR is robust across generating order (a permutation always fully decorrelates), so the intended contrast->0 worst case is not reached; near-white generation and directional/anisotropic curvature are untested. Fixed low-order fit, not a learned patch (GAP #2 proper open). clifford_fir readout not yet compared.
next_experiment_authorized: the LEARNED ChebyCR/Clifford-FIR patch + entropy feedback (GAP #2 proper) attached to THIS metric (exact scalar solve provably insufficient), OR the near-white / anisotropic worst-case stress before that.
next_experiment_proposal: wire clifford_fir over the plaquette cycle pool as a second closed-form readout and a learned-patch arm; measure both against the fixed ChebyCR ceiling on a near-white-contrast sweep so the learned method faces a non-saturated gap.
